cards.title: Finding Family: Couple Adopts Baby Found on Subway
cards.body: A social worker discovered an abandoned newborn on a New York City subway in 2000 and decided to adopt him. Now 26 years old, the child has become an integral part of their family.

cards.title: Emotionally Mature Parenting: Raising Healthy Children
cards.body: Psychologist Lindsay C Gibson shares advice on raising happy, healthy children without repeating patterns of emotional immaturity. Her book became an unexpected pandemic bestseller.

cards.title: Parenting a Teenager: A Guide to A-Levels
cards.body: Parents of teenagers taking A-levels face unique challenges when trying to support their learning. The emotional toll of holding flashcards for complex subjects is real.

cards.title: Foster Parenting: What You Need to Know
cards.body: Foster parenting requires more than love—it demands a trauma-informed approach to help children heal. Understanding the emotional needs of foster children is essential.

cards.title: College Anxiety: When Students Fear Medical Care
cards.body: Many college students struggle with anxiety around medical appointments, preventing them from seeking necessary care. Addressing this fear is critical for their long-term health.

cards.title: Paternal Postpartum Depression: A Missed Diagnosis
cards.body: Fathers can experience postpartum depression, but it often goes unrecognized and untreated. Raising awareness helps parents get the support they need.

cards.title: Overprotective Parenting Rooted in Childhood Trauma
cards.body: Parents who grew up without consistent safety may become hyper-vigilant protectors of their own children. Understanding this pattern helps break the cycle.

cards.title: Fatherhood: Embracing Imperfection for Legacy
cards.body: Fathers who embrace their past and present flaws can create a more loving intergenerational legacy. Self-awareness becomes the foundation for healing with adult children.

cards.title: Fathers and Self-Awareness: Healing Adult Relationships
cards.body: Self-awareness can become the foundation for reconciliation and renewed intimacy between fathers and their young adult children. Healing starts with honest reflection.
